@30846  = ~Splint mail is a variant of banded mail in which the metal strips are applied vertically to the backing of chain, leather, or cloth rather than horizontally as in banded mail. Since the body does not swivel in mid-torso as much as it flexes back to front, splint mail is more restrictive in battle. This suit of armor is enchanted, granting the wearer an additional bonus of +1 to their Armor Class.

STATISTICS:

Armor Class: 3 (2 vs. piercing and missile, 1 vs. crushing)
Requires:
 8 Strength

Weight: 10《直行鏈甲是鎖子甲的一種變體。它的鎖鏈和鋼環是以垂直的方式加在衣物和盔甲之前，與其他盔甲慣用的水平方向不同。因為人類的身體其實比較常做垂直方向的運動，所以它在戰鬥時所受到的局限較大。這套盔甲施有魔法，使穿戴者的防禦等級獲得+1的加權。

資料資料：

防禦等級：3（對穿刺和遠程攻擊為2，對鈍擊攻擊為1）
需求：

@31458  = ~I am bid to write this by those we serve.

You who carry out the work, I am your humble servant. When at last you need the final ingredient for the grand project, I shall be the one that brings it to you. Seek me out in the hills of Umar and signal your identity with the purchase of the book "The History of the Zhentarim." This will identify you, for I do not know your name, nor should I. The need for security is paramount, so you must also utter my secret name, constructed from the name of your immediate master. There will be no mistake with this method.

Our names are the same in their scale and their cut. 
The last of his begins just as the first of mine.
The second of my first is the second of his first, though regressed a full four steps.
The third of my first is four more than the last of his first.
The entire last of my first is the last of his first, except the first one of these is the first of my last.
The first of his last is mine plus one.
The next of my last is the last of his first plus one.
The last of my last are third and second, coming in first for him.

I am sorry for such games, but it is certain that only you shall know this. I await your arrival.《這是受我所侍奉的主之托而寫的。
執行工作的你，我是你謙卑的僕人。當你需要最後的材料時，我會把它送給你。請到烏瑪山丘上來找我，帶著「散塔林會的歷史」這本書，好讓我辨認你的身份，因為我不認識你，也沒有必要知道你的名字。保密非常重要，因此你還必須說出我的秘密名字，絕不可出錯。我的秘密名字是來自於你目前主人的名字。這樣做就不會出錯。

我們的名字在樣式與斷句上相同。
他的姓的第一個字母和我的名字的第一個字母相同。
我的名字的第二個字母是他名字的第二個字母，但後溯了四步。
我的名字的第三個字母比他的名字的最後一個字母多了四。
我的名字所餘的整個部分就是他的名字所餘的整個部分，但是第一個字母是我的姓的第一個字母。
他的姓的第一個字母是我的加一。
我的姓的次一個是他的名字的最後一個字母加一。
我的姓的最後兩個字是他的名的第三和第二個。

我很抱歉必須玩這種遊戲，但是毫無疑問，只有你知道。我恭候你的駕臨。》~

@31853  = ~~~~~Dradeel's Recipe:

MONKEY BALLS (makes ~40)
A) 1/2 c. peanut butter
     1/3 c. honey
     1/2 c. granola
     1/2 c. crushed "cheery" cereal
     dash      nutmeg
     dash      cinnamon
     1/2 c. oatmeal

B) 1 c.   sugar
     1/4 c. cocoa
     1/4 c. butter
     1/4 c. milk
     1/4 tsp. vanilla

INSTRUCTIONS:
    Mix ingredients in (A), except oatmeal. Roll into small balls about the diameter of a coin. Coat balls in oatmeal.
    Boil ingredients in (B) together for 3-1/2 to 4 minutes. Take from heat. Dip balls in chocolate using spoon or toothpick. Chill and serve.

    Monkey balls are to be served with the following poem on prominent display:

"Monkey Balls"
These sweet and gracious monkey balls,
Served on plates and sold from stalls,
Bring madness to these hallowed halls
Of urban academe.

Huzzaks for chocolate monkey balls!
We'll eat them 'til the ceiling falls,
'Til Time herself tears down these walls
Of urban academe.

So buy yourself a monkey ball,
Savour it and then stand tall,
A bulwark 'gainst the deaf'ning call
Of urban academe.
                    —by Ergo the organ-grinder 《德拉迪爾的食譜：
猴球（約四十顆）

A）	1/2c. 		花生衣
			1/3c. 		蜂蜜
			1/2c. 		麵粉
			1/2c. 		壓碎的「cheery」麥片
			少許				肉豆蔻
			少許				肉桂皮
			1/2c. 		燕麥片

B）	1c. 			糖
			1/4c. 		可可
			1/4c. 		奶油
			1/4c. 		牛奶
			1/4茶匙	香草

步驟

			將材料（A）混合（燕麥片除外）。
			捲成小球，直徑約為一個銅板大小。把小球放進燕麥片裡。
			將材料（B）烹煮三分半至四分鐘。離火。
			用湯匙或牙籤將小球沾上巧克力。
			冷卻後上桌。

猴球上桌時，應朗讀以下的詩，或將之置於明顯的地方：

「猴球」

這些香甜而美麗的猴球，
以盤提供，在攤位上出售，
為城市大學的神聖殿堂
帶來瘋狂。

巧克力猴球萬歲！
我們要享用它，直到屋子塌下，
直到時間拆毀這些牆
城市大學的牆。

所以為你自己買個猴球，
品嚐它，然後站立，
對抗城市大學震耳欲聾的呼喊
的精神堡壘。

－－作者：風琴手厄過》~~~~~
@31854  = ~Dradeel's Recipe: 
Amnian Dragoon Soup

4 cups of beef broth
8 cups of water
2 leeks, sliced thin
2 carrots, sliced
1 celery stalk, thinly sliced
1 small cabbage, shredded
1 large onion, sliced
1 garlic clove, minced
2 lemons
4 eggs
salt and pepper to taste

1) Pour the broth and the water into a large pot. Add all the vegetables and bring to a gentle boil. Let the fire burn to a low-medium heat and simmer the soup for 1-1/2 hours, covered. Add more water if needed. Skim all of the white part that rises in the soup and discard.

2) Squeeze the juice from the 2 lemons and add it to the soup. Beat the eggs carefully and mix them with the soup. Add the seasonings and stir quickly for 2-3 minutes. Serve immediately.《德拉迪爾的食譜：
安姆龍湯

4杯牛肉湯
8杯水
2根蔥，切薄
2根紅蘿蔔，切片
1顆芹菜，切薄片
1小顆包心菜，切碎
1大顆洋蔥，切片
1顆蒜，切碎
2個檸檬
4個蛋
鹽和胡椒

1）	將牛肉湯和水倒進大鍋裡。將所有蔬菜倒進去，中火燉煮一至一個半小時，若有必要則加水。
		濾除湯中所有白色的成份。

2）	擠出兩顆檸檬的汁，加入湯中。將蛋打散，混入湯中。加入調味料，迅速攪動二至三分鐘。立刻上桌。》~
@31855  = ~Dradeel's Recipe:
Baldur's Delight
Sweet treats from the fabled kitchen of Mother Baldur.
 
5 tablespoons cornstarch 
1/2 cup chilled water 
1/2 cup hot water 
1/2 cup orange juice
2 cups sugar 
1 teaspoon lemon juice (or an exotic, such as rosewater) 
2 cups of nuts (again, as exotic as you like) 
A portion of powdered sugar or some other topical candy sprinkle

Mix the cornstarch thoroughly with the cold water and set to the side.
Blend the hot water, orange juice, and sugar together, bringing the mixture to a boil.
To this, add the cornstarch and simmer for 15 minutes, stirring often.

Remove from heat, stir in the nuts, and add the lemon juice or your alternate flavoring. 
Pour the entire mixture into a buttered or greased pan and chill. 

When set, cut sections of about an inch and roll in the powdered sugar or sprinkles to prevent it sticking.

Enjoy.《德拉迪爾的食譜
博德之樂：
傳說中的博德之母廚房所提供的甜點。

5湯匙玉米粉
1/2杯冰水
1/2杯熱水
1/2杯柳橙汁
2杯糖
1茶匙檸檬汁（或特別味道的汁，例如玫瑰水）
2杯花生（同樣地，你可以隨便放）
一份糖粉或其它糖果粉。

將玉米粉和冷水充份混合，置於一旁備用。
將熱水，柳橙汁和糖混合，將混合汁煮開。
加入玉米粉慢煮十五分鐘，不停地攪拌。

關火，加入花生及檸檬汁或你選擇的味道。
將整個混合液倒進抹好奶油的盤子上，冷卻。

定形後，切成約一吋的小塊，沾上糖粉或其它糖果粉，以免黏住。

希望你喜歡。》~

@31857  = ~Dradeel's Recipe:
A cookie to reinforce the fortitude, from the kitchen of a particularly shaggy friend:

Ruby Racks:

1 cup of dry oatmeal 
2 cups of flour
1/4 pound of butter
2 eggs 
1/2 cup of cocoa
1/2 cup of sugar
1 tablespoon vanilla
1 teaspoon walnut extract

While oven preheats to 350, combine all ingredients in a large bowl, adding a small amount of milk if the mixture is too dry to properly blend. Add extra sugar if the batter is not to your liking at this point. Roll into balls and press on a greased pan. Bake for 10-15 minutes, depending on the thickness of your portions and your personal preference for consistency. 

Jinkies, they're good!《德拉迪爾的食譜：
補充精神的餅乾，來自一位特別不修邊幅的朋友的廚房：

紅寶石餅乾：

1杯乾燕麥片
2杯麵粉
1/4磅奶油
2個蛋
1/2杯可可
1/2杯糖
1茶匙香草
1茶匙胡桃干

將爐預熱至350度，將所有材料混入大碗中，如果混合物過干，可加入少量牛奶。如果喜歡甜食，可於此時加入更多糖。切成球狀，在抹油的盤子上壓平。烤十至十五分鐘，依你的餅乾厚度和個人喜好而定。

金吉斯，這個好吃！》~

@31865  = ~The Vampiricus Omnibus: Unabridged
Ending the Plague of Teeth
This tome is quite ancient, and you do not recognize the tongue in which it is written. Fortunately, Bodhi has scrawled a translation of selected passages in the margins, as well as further questions she apparently wished to ask on the subject.

"Stuat tuo Gotha: 
The resolution:
With the witch at the fore, long previous to her treachery, we did stride the hills of Umar seeking comfort in our hour of need. The time had come for the living to mourn no more, and the blades that were fettered by sentiment were cut free, and it was decided that we would speak for our lives with the sinews of our sword arms. The honored dead that did take flight in the darkness of shadow would no more receive sorrow from those of us that had lost their friends and relatives to the plague of teeth. 

Mornat de'pas Golvana Fathorn:
Battles when came the dead:
There came we, the army of light, armed with faith and conviction, and we did march unto the tombs of the restless dead during hours of day. With wooden edge, we struck down master and slave, though many paid for each thrust with their lives. In the fallen, there was the danger of a scourge anew, and many of the newly infected seemed doomed to walk again with dire purpose. 

Aegato Davon:
The plea:
'No more,' cried we! No more would we lose to the plague. These new fallen could not be abandoned. We had fought for our lives and theirs, and they would not be taken. To the Temple we went, to the god of Sun and Light. Amaunator would save them. Amaunator, whose touch turns shadows to fire. In the arms of the Sun god were the infected placed, and the hearts of their dark masters were laid there with them. Blood did burn, and the dead returned, but not as undead or unliving, but alive and freed from taint. Many did walk the light side home, when last the plague did fall.

Relevance of archaic worship of Amaunator?
Origin of vampiric plague?
Any link to a modern cure to be worried of?
Historical document or historical fiction?"

A final note is scribbled... 

"*Interrogate Oghma monk at Docks District temple about further information. He should be able to provide more information on reversing vampirism*"

STATISTICS:

Weight: 2《吸血鬼百科
結束利牙之災

這本書冊相當古老，你也不認得它所使用的語言。幸運的是，芭荻在書緣上寫下一些訊息的翻譯，以及她顯然想要發問的進一步問題。

史圖亞特·托·戈撒：
決心：
在女巫率先的情況下（那是在她背叛之前很久的事），我們攻進烏瑪的山丘，在我們需要的時刻尋找慰藉。生者不再呻吟的時候來到了，被感性所束縛的刀劍獲得了自由，而且我們決定，要用握劍的力量來為我們的生命說話。在陰影的黑暗中逃跑的死者不會再感受到我們因為失去朋友和親屬的悲痛。

莫內·德'帕斯·戈瓦那·法東：
死者來時的戰鬥：
我們來了，光明之軍，裝備著信仰和信心，我們確實在白天時進入不眠死者的墳墓。我們用木椿擊倒主人和奴隸，雖然有許多人在刺擊時付出他們的生命。在倒下的屍體中，仍有可能再度爬起，而且有許多剛受感染的人似乎注定要再度行走在世上。

艾加多·達文：
跳蚤：
「別再來了」我們叫喊著。我們不要再死於瘟疫中。這些剛死去的人不能把他們丟棄。我們為自己和他們的性命戰鬥，他們不能被帶走。我們前往神殿，去見太陽與光明之神。阿曼納塔會救他們。阿曼納塔，他的接觸可以使鬼魂變成火焰。將受感染的人放在太陽神的懷抱裡，他們的黑暗主人的心放在裡面。血燒了起來，死者返回，但是不再是不死者，而是活生生的人，沒有污穢。人們在光中走回家，瘟疫失敗了。

對阿曼納塔崇拜的起源？
吸血鬼瘟疫的起源？
有沒有任何現代的治療方法需要留意？
有歷史文件或小說嗎？

STATISTICS:

Weight: 2《當你在手中緩緩地轉動這隻大高腳杯時，杯上的銀漆閃閃發亮。冷硬的杯子上簡潔地刻著：「無懼的安納格」。這只聖盃表彰了他對至熱之心騎士團的忠誠奉獻。由於它象徵著安納格的榮耀，如果失去了聖盃，對安納格而言，簡直比失去聖武士的資格還要更加地讓他氣憤難平。

(Necromancy)

Level: 3
Range: 35 ft.
Duration: 2 rounds/level
Casting Time: 3
Area of Effect: 1 creature and any enemies within 4 ft.
Saving Throw: Neg.

This spell holds 1d4 undead creatures rigidly immobile and in place. The effect is centered on the victim selected by the caster. Any enemies within 4 ft. of the target are also affected. Those who succeed on their Saving Throws are totally unaffected by the spell. Held creatures cannot move or speak, but they remain aware of events around them and can use abilities not requiring motion or speech. Being held does not prevent the worsening of the subject's condition due to wounds.《不死生物定身術（死靈系）
等級：3
有效距離：施法者視線所及
持續時間：2輪/每等級
施法時間：3
影響範圍：特殊
豁免檢定：通過則無效

@32428  = ~Mass Invisibility

Level: 7
Range: Visual range of the caster

Casting Time: 7

This spell is similar to the Invisibility spell, but the recipient is able to attack, either by missile discharge, melee combat, or spellcasting, and remain unseen. Note, however, that there are sometimes telltale traces, a shimmering, so that an observant opponent can attack the invisible spell recipient. These traces are only noticeable when specifically looked for (after the invisible character has made <PRO_HISHER> presence known). Attacks against the invisible character suffer -4 penalties to the attack rolls, and the invisible character's Saving Throws are made with a +4 bonus.《集體隱形術（幻術/魅影系）

有效距離：施法者視線所及
持續時間：1輪/每等級
施法時間：7
影響範圍：30尺方圓
豁免檢定：無

集體隱形術可讓作用範圍內的所有友方生物都受到高等隱形術的保護。此法術和隱形術十分類似，但是受術者依舊可以進行攻擊，不論是使用遠程武器、近身戰鬥，還是施展法術，都可繼續保持隱形的狀態。不過要注意，輕微的氣流震動還是可能暴露出位置，使敵人得以攻擊隱形者，但是這些跡象只有特別注意才會發現（當隱形的人物暴露出蹤跡之後）。攻擊隱形的人物將會受到命中檢定-4的不利修正，而隱形生物的豁免檢定則擁有+4的優勢修正。

@32429  = ~Invisibility, 10' Radius
(Illusion/Phantasm)

Level: 3
Range: 0
Duration: Special 
Casting Time: 9
Area of Effect: 10-ft. radius
Saving Throw: None 

This spell causes all friendly creatures within 10 ft. of the caster to vanish from sight and be undetectable by normal vision or even infravision. Of course, the invisible creature(s) are not magically silenced, and certain other conditions can render the creature(s) detectable. Even allies cannot see the invisible creature(s) or their gear, unless these allies can normally see invisible things or employ magic to do so. Items dropped or put down by the invisible creature(s) become visible; items picked up disappear if tucked into the clothing or pouches worn by the creature(s). The spell remains in effect until it is magically broken or dispelled, until the wizard or recipient cancels it, until the recipient attacks any creature, or until 24 hours have passed. The invisible being(s) cannot open doors, talk, eat, climb stairs, etc. If they attack, they immediately become visible, although the invisibility enables them to attack first.《十尺隱形（幻術/魅影系）
等級：3
有效距離：0
持續時間：特殊
施法時間：1輪
影響範圍：10尺方圓
豁免檢定：無

@33347  = ~Your history is nearly as unknown as your future, and the things that are certain seem more fancy than fact. As unlikely as it may seem, you have the blood of a deity coursing through your veins.

You are a product of the Time of Troubles, a cataclysmic period when the gods were made flesh and forced to walk the earth among their followers. One such deity foresaw both the event and his inevitable death because of it, and so took steps to effect his resurrection. This god strode the land before he was made to, and in his wake left a score of mortal progeny driven to conquer and rule. They were not intended to be his successors, but rather the fuel for his rebirth. Heroes would rise to counter these tyrants, and when their evil fell, it would fall to the father.

The god was Bhaal, Lord of Murder, and you are one of his children.

You were cared for as a child by Gorion, a powerful wizard that may have had even more powerful friends. It was his influence that allowed you to spend your youth in the library fortress of Candlekeep, where the resident monks schooled you in your skills. Children were an oddity at the keep, though you did have a friend in Imoen. She seemed a kindred spirit, though you knew no more of her background than your own. Gorion never explained how you or she came to be there, or why you needed such a secluded home. It was only after his death that you learned the truth about your bloodline, when you were forced into conflict with his killer, another child of Bhaal.

Sarevok was this sibling's name, and he had embraced his foul origins, determined to exploit them and become the next Lord of Murder. He sought to create death on a massive scale, a war of sacrifice that would prove his claim to his father's throne. You unraveled his carefully wrought plans, and ultimately it was you that took his life, sending his taint back to Bhaal. A victory of sorts...

Now you face an uncertain future. A child of murder, you have a lineage that will tempt the ignorant to fear you and the unscrupulous to use you. And always the essence of Bhaal is within, exerting its dark pull when you are weakest. Which is the greater fear: Losing your life to fuel the fire, or losing your will and becoming it?《你的過去幾乎和你的未來一樣不可知，而且確定的事似乎比較像是幻想，而不像事實。而且雖然看起來不大可能，但是你的血液裡確實流動著神祇的血統。

你是混亂時代的產品，那時候眾神成為肉身，被迫與其信徒一同行走在人間。有一個神祇預見這個事件，以及他因此而不可避免的死亡，因此設法安排自己的復活。這個神祇在他被逐入大地之前，就來到地上，並且令一些凡人子孫，競相征服及統治。他們不是要成為他的繼承者，而是推動他的重生。將有英雄出現來征服這些暴君，當他們的邪行失敗時，也就是其父親失敗之時。

這個神祇就是巴爾，殺戮之主，而你就是他的子孫之一。

你被葛立安撫養長大，他是一位強大的法師，而且擁有更加強大的朋友。憑著他的影響力，你的青少年時期得以在燭堡的圖書館堡壘中度過，那裡的修道士教導你所需的技能。在那裡很少看到小孩，雖然你確實交了一個朋友，叫做愛蒙。她可以說是志趣相投的人，只不過你對她一樣一無所知。葛立安從來不曾解釋過你們是怎麼到那裡去的，或是為什麼你們要住在這麼封閉的地方。一直到他死後，你才知道你的血統真相，並且被迫面對殺死他的兇手，另一個巴爾子嗣。

這個惡棍名叫沙洛佛克，他接受了他的邪惡來源，定義要成為下一個毀滅之王。他意圖造成大規模的死亡，引發犧牲的戰爭，以證明他有權繼承其父的王座。你破壞了他的計劃，最後還取走他的性命，讓他回到巴爾的身邊去。算是某種勝利吧……

現在你面對著不確定的未來。你是謀殺之子，你的血統令無知者懼怕你，不道德的人想要利用你。而且巴爾的本質一直在你裡面，當你軟弱時，就發揮它的黑暗力量。更大的恐懼在於：要失去你的生命以熄滅這團火焰，還是失去你的意志，成為它的一部分？》~

@34017  = ~You skip many of the entries of times and places far removed from your current worries.

Flamerule 7
Kaatje came to visit again. A sweet child, eager to learn my ways with the wild creatures.

Flamerule 12
Dark creatures roam the forests. Several townspeople have been slain by some unknown predator. Everyone seems to have their own opinion of what is doing the killing. The strangest thing is that the bodies are disappearing in the morning. This leaves me baffled, for most signs point to a pack of wolves that have been in the area for a long time. Wolves don't steal bodies.

Flamerule 13
Or perhaps I should say wolf-like creatures. At night while I've been patrolling, I've often caught shadowy glimpses of these "wolves" running alongside me. I've tried to attract their attention, but they ignore me. I find this most strange, as if the pack is being controlled by someone else. Years ago, I spoke with the pack leaders, and they were cordial. Now they either flee from me or, and I fear to say this, they stalk me.

Flamerule 21
It is near noon and still the wood outside my cabin seems full of shadows. I've been hard-pressed to hear birds, and most of the larger animals have long fled. I plan to find the wolves' den (this afternoon). I've drawn a map to it. All signs point to them as being the culprits, but what wolves act like these? They are wolves with the cunning of men.

And there is something else, a whispering in my mind. It is faint now, in the waking hours, but while I sleep, I dream only of this voice and the face behind it. Whether it is connected to the voices, I do not know. I suspect by the time that the sun sets tonight I'll have answers to my questions.《你略過許多和你目前擔心的事情無關的記錄。

七月七日
凱堤再度來訪。她是個乖巧的小孩，急著學習我在野生動物方面的知識。

七月十二日
黑暗生物在森林裡遊蕩。好幾個村民都被不明的掠食者所殺。關於殺戮者，似乎每個人都有自己的看法。最奇怪的是，屍體在早上都會消失。這讓我感到困惑：因為大部分的跡象都指向一群早已在這區裡出沒的狼群；而狼卻不會偷走屍體。

七月十三日
也許我應該說是「像狼」的生物吧！晚上我在巡邏時，我經常瞄到這些「狼群」的身影就在附近出沒。我試著吸引它們的注意力，但是它們不理我。我發現這件事很奇怪，好像那群狼被別人控制住一樣。多年以前，我曾和狼群領袖交談過，它們很和善。但現在它們卻會躲避我，或者，我不敢這麼說，它們是在跟蹤我。

七月廿一日
時近中午，我的小屋外面的樹林裡似乎仍然滿是陰影。我知道鳥類和較大的動物都已經逃走了。我打算找出狼群的巢穴（今天下午）；我已經畫了一張地圖。所有的跡象都指出它們就是罪犯，但是什麼狼會有這種行為？它們是擁有人類智慧的狼。
還有別的怪事：我的腦海中的微聲。在我清醒時聲音就變小了，但是當我一睡著，我就一定會夢見這個聲音，還有它之後的面孔。它是否和聲音有關，我並不知道。我猜想，當今晚日落之後，我就會得到我的答案。》~

@34090  = ~Lawrence Lilarcor was well known, not for being brave, but for being an idiot. As the tale goes, the boastful Lilarcor left his village at the urging of his friends so that the "great hero" could do battle with a devious treant. He walked for days in the dead of winter until, feverish, he found his target and began an epic wrestling match. Unfortunately (or perhaps luckily), the "treant" was nothing more than a craggy old normal oak. His friends had been jesting, not actually expecting that Lilarcor would go fight the fictitiously dangerous tree. That might have been the end of it, but Lilarcor, not really knowing what a treant was in the first place, didn't realize the truth. He eventually uprooted the oak and, marching proudly home, he declared himself a hero. Thus was born a laughing stock of epic proportions, and over time the name of Lilarcor became the sacrificial fool in many tales of "less than brilliance."

It is not known whether this enchanted weapon is Lilarcor himself, perhaps imprisoned by an evil mage or some other odd coincidence of fate, but it certainly acts in a manner consistent with his level of competence. If it is he, he has never bemoaned his captivity. He might not realize, or care, that he is no longer a human.

As a weapon, Lilarcor has its uses, but many a warrior has eventually given it away. Banter such as "Ouch, that musta hurt," "Oh yeah! Got 'im good," and "Beware my bite for it might... might... might really hurt or something" is a constant barrage on a warrior's psyche.

STATISTICS:

Equipped abilities:
– Immunity to charm
– Immunity to confusion

THAC0: +3
Damage: 1d10+3 (slashing)
Speed Factor: 8
Proficiency Type: Two-handed Sword
Type: Two-handed
Requires:
 14 Strength

Weight: 10《勞倫斯·利拉寇爾名聲遠播，不是因為他的英勇事跡，而是因為他是個白癡。早在他還是個男孩時，他的事跡就已被吟遊詩人傳唱。利拉寇爾在「朋友」的慫恿之下，離開小鎮北行，要去對付一個狡詐的樹人。利拉寇爾在寒冬中走了兩天兩夜，找到樹人，展開一場史詩般壯烈的戰鬥。不幸的是（也許是幸運），他的對手只是一般的樹，他被人愚弄了。大家心想，他該不會和一棵樹戰鬥吧？他卻仍深信不疑，把樹連根拔起，砍成碎片，然後返回家鄉，宣稱自己是英雄，從此成為史詩中的笑料來源，漸漸地，利拉寇爾這個名字成為許多「不聰明」故事中，富有犧牲精神的雙子的名字。
至於這把武器是否真為利拉寇爾，是否由於某個邪惡法師的魔法或命運的巧合而被囚禁於內，就不得而知了。不過它的行為倒真的和他的能力成正比。如果這真是那位利拉寇爾，他也從未為自己被囚禁的厄運而哀痛。他可能並沒有發現，或是不在乎，自己已不再是人類。
「利拉寇爾」這把劍倒是頗有用，不過許多戰士寧願將它送人。諸如「噢！那一定很痛！」「喔！上吧！」「小心我的刺激，可能會……會……會真的很痛！」此類的評語不斷無情地敲打戰士的心靈。

Weight: 4《基本護具，用以蠟或水加強的厚皮革製成。它是堅固的保護，不像皮靴或外套那麼輕便。像那樣薄的皮革提供的保護不會比一般衣服好到那裡去。魔法提升這件護甲的保護能力。

Casting Time: 9

This is the reverse of the spell Imprisonment. When a creature is imprisoned, <PRO_HESHE> is trapped in a sphere far beneath the surface of the earth. If this spell is cast in the area where <PRO_HESHE> was imprisoned, it will instantly free <PRO_HIMHER> and <PRO_HESHE> will reappear exactly where <PRO_HESHE> was standing when <PRO_HESHE> was imprisoned. This spell will also cancel the effects of the spell Maze and return any characters trapped in the labyrinth of planes.《自由術（防護系）
等級：9
有效距離：特殊
持續時間：立即
施法時間：9
影響範圍：特殊
豁免檢定：無

注意：在有人被監禁的區域施行此法術，就可以將他們釋放。
此為禁錮術的反向。當某生物遭禁錮時，等於是被困在地球地底的某個領域。如果就在該生物所在的區域施行此法術，他就會立刻重獲自由並且重新出現在當初被監禁的位置。此法術也可抵銷迷宮術的效果，讓困在異界迷宮中的生物得以返回。》~

(Necromancy)

The caster of the spell acts as a conduit between the two planes, sucking life from a victim and transferring it to the Negative Energy Plane, draining <PRO_HIMHER> of 2 levels of experience. The target of this spell loses levels, Hit Dice, Hit Points, and abilities permanently. These levels can only be restored by a priest's Restoration spell.《吸能術（死靈系）

領域：死靈
有效距離：碰觸
持續時間：永久
施法時間：7
影響範圍：目標生物
豁免檢定：無

@35604  = ~Greater Restoration
(Necromancy)

Level: 7
Sphere: Necromantic
Range: Touch
Duration: Permanent
Casting Time: 3
Area of Effect: 1 creature
Saving Throw: None 

When this spell is cast, the life energy level of the recipient is raised. This reverses any previous life energy level drain of the creature by a force or monster. A Greater Restoration spell will also restore the Intelligence score of a creature affected by the Feeblemind spell and negate any form of insanity, such as confusion or berserk. It fully heals the target and cures any diseases or poisons. The casting of this spell is very draining on the priest, and <PRO_HESHE> will likely require rest immediately afterward.《強力復原術（死靈系）
等級：7
領域：死靈
有效距離：碰觸
持續時間：永久
施法時間：3
影響範圍：目標生物
豁免檢定：無

此法術施行後，受術者的生命能量等級會提高。也就是說，此法術可復原之前遭神秘力量或怪物吸取的生命能量等級。此外，復原術也能夠讓之前因弱智術而智力降低的受術者恢復智力。它還能抵銷任一形式的瘋狂狀態（如困惑或狂暴）、治療受術者，為其治病或解毒等。不過，施行此法對牧師而言相當費力，他必須在施法後立刻休息，因為施法後的疲勞度與一日的疲勞上限相當。》~

Uttering an Unholy Word spell creates magic of tremendous power. The priest acts as a bridge between <PRO_HISHER> god and the Prime Material Plane, causing a small explosion centered on the priest and reaching up to a 15-ft. radius. It affects only creatures of any good alignment that are caught in the area of effect. The effects vary depending on the target's Hit Dice:

Hit Dice / Levels  —  Effects of Unholy Word
Less than 4  —  Death
4 to 7  —  Stunned for 1 turn
8 to 11  —  Slowed for 1 turn with a 75% chance of spell failure
12 and up  —  Deafened for 1 turn with a 50% chance of spell failure

@35963  = ~The katana is a single-edged, slightly curved sword that ends with a chisel point. The katana is perhaps one of the finest swords ever made, the steps to its creation a long and secret process that is more art form than anything else. This blade is an important facet of Kara-Turan culture. To touch or wield a samurai's katana is to insult the samurai and to incite his wrath. For a gaijin, or foreigner, to own such a blade is a rare occurrence. Either the gaijin is a greatly honored friend of an important minister, or the gaijin has murdered a samurai.

STATISTICS:

Damage: 1d10 (slashing)
Speed Factor: 4
Proficiency Type: Katana
Type: One-handed
Requires:

@36123  = ~<CHARNAME>,

Goodbye. No sappy farewells or crying over how things must be; I do neither of these things well, nor would I wish to. I have made a decision, one that is not ideal, but it is necessary. I can go nowhere without this shadow, and I wish to leave it behind. I am returning to the Harper Hold that I might face judgment there. I will plead the case as we saw it for whatever they accuse, and if they do not see reason, then I will suffer what I must. Galvarey had detractors as well as allies, so I believe I can end this in my favor. If not, at least it will be an end.
 
I do not ask you to follow. I do this for myself, and there will be little or no gain for you. You may still be a target of violence, but perhaps their interest in you will wither beside the capture of a "traitor."

For better or worse, I am taking control once more. I hope to see you again in better times. 

With nature's love and mine,
Jaheira《<CHARNAME>：

再會了。我們不需要感傷的道別，或是為了必然的事情而流淚；我並不擅長這些事，我也不想做。我做了一個決定；不算理想的決定，但卻是必要的。無論我到了那裡，這個陰影都會跟著我，而我想要擺脫它。我正在回豎琴手基地的路上，也許我在那裡得要面對審判。我會以我們親眼所見的真相來對他們所做的指控提出辯解，而如果他們還是不能接受，我就得要承受必要的苦痛。蓋佛瑞雖有黨羽，但也有人不齒他的作為，因此我相信我可以順利地結束這件事。如果不能，至少是結束了。

我不要求你一起來。我是為了自己才這樣做的，對你來說幾乎沒有一點好處。你仍然可能是他們暴力相向的目標，但是在抓到「叛徒」之後，也許他們對你的興趣會減退一些。

無論如何，我又再次取得主控權囉！我希望能在較好的時機裡再見到你。

自然和我的愛永遠與你同在。

賈希拉》~
